topics: Course Objectives: Familiarize with various tools, measuring devices, practices, and methods employed in the industry
Course Objectives: Apply workshop experience while developing products or projects for the benefit of society
Introduction to workshop practice, safety precautions, shop floor ethics, and basic first aid knowledge

Study of mechanical and measurement tools, components, and their applications (screw drivers, spanners, Allen keys, cutting pliers, bearings, seals, O-rings, circlips, keys, Vernier calipers, height gauge, depth gauge, micrometers, bevel protractor)
Carpentry: Understanding carpentry tools and practicing joints (T-Lap, Cross Lap, Dovetail, and Mortise)
Foundry: Understanding foundry tools and practicing bench moulding, floor moulding, core making, and pattern making
Sheet metal: Understanding working tools and practicing cylindrical, conical, and prismatic shapes
Fitting: Understanding fitting tools and practicing square joint, V-joint, and male-female fitting
Plumbing: Understanding plumbing tools and pipe joints, and practicing pipe joining using at least three types of joints
Smithy: Understanding smithy tools and demonstrating forgeability of different materials (MS, Al, alloy steel, cast steel) in cold and hot states, including one smithy exercise on a square prism
Welding: Understanding welding equipment and practicing electric arc welding, including bead formation in horizontal, vertical, and overhead positions
Rolling: Objective of rolling, rolling process, and practical on a two high rolling mill
Electroplating a given job

Metrology: Common measuring instruments, experiments to find the angle of a dovetail, angle of a taper, and radius of a circular surface, and introduction to Vernier bevel protractor, Vernier depth gauge, and Vernier height gauge
Assembly: Demonstration of dissembling and assembling cylinder and piston assembly, tail stock assembly, bicycle, and pump or any other machine
Machines: Demonstration of shaping and slotting machine, milling machine, grinding machine, lathe, and drilling machine
Modern manufacturing methods: Demonstration of power tools, CNC machine tools, 3D printing, and soft materials cutting using special machines
Use of proper personal protective equipments and measurements using tape, ruler, vernier calipers, and screw gauge
Measuring the area of a plot with an irregular boundary using a chain and cross staff
Measuring the area of a building using distomat
Finding the level difference between two points using dumpy level
Onsite quality assessment of brick and cement
Construct a 1 and 1 1/2 thick brick wall (height 50 cm, minimum length 60 cm) using English bond and checking verticality
Construct a 1 and 1 1/2 thick brick wall (height 50 cm, minimum length 60 cm) using Flemish bond and checking verticality
Estimate the number of different types of building blocks needed to construct the walls of a room measuring 2m x 3m, accounting for standard-sized doors and windows
Setting out of a two roomed building using thread, tape, and water tube levelling

Conduct a market study to understand the types, prices, and general specifications of at least three materials available in the market (bricks, cement, aggregates, steel, plumbing items, fixtures, welding rods, fasteners, etc.)
Studying the tools and testing instruments for electrical works and wiring a light or fan circuit using one way and two-way switch

Familiarization and application of testing instruments and commonly used tools in electronic works (multimeter, soldering iron, de-soldering pump, pliers, cutters, wire strippers, screw drivers, tweezers, crimping tool, hot air soldering and desoldering station)
